message:
fix: G.copy() is shallow per nx contract (br-r37-c1-3tlkj)

fnx.{Graph,DiGraph,MultiGraph,MultiDiGraph}.copy() did a deep copy of
all node, edge, and graph-level attributes via copy.deepcopy. nx
documents and implements a *shallow* copy:

  > The copy method by default returns an independent shallow copy
  > of the graph and attributes. That is, if an attribute is a
  > container, that container is shared by the original and the copy.
  >   — networkx.Graph.copy docstring

Repro:
    >>> G = fnx.Graph(); G.add_node(1, payload={'list': [1,2,3]})
    >>> H = G.copy()
    >>> H.nodes[1]['payload'] is G.nodes[1]['payload']
    False    # nx says True
    >>> H.nodes[1]['payload']['list'].append(99)
    >>> G.nodes[1]['payload']['list']
    [1, 2, 3]    # nx returns [1, 2, 3, 99]

Drop-in code that relies on shared inner-container mutation (a common
pattern for caching layers and incremental analysis pipelines)
silently broke under fnx.

Fix
---
``_copy_preserving_insertion_order`` in ``python/franken_networkx/__init__.py``
now mirrors nx.Graph.copy structurally:
- ``result.graph.update(self.graph)`` instead of
  ``result.graph.update(deepcopy(self.graph))``.
- ``dict(attrs)`` (a shallow copy of the outer attr dict) instead of
  ``deepcopy(attrs)`` for nodes and edges.

The outer attrs dicts (G.nodes[n], G[u][v], G.graph) remain
*independent* between original and copy — the shallow copy only
shares the *values* stored at each top-level attr key, exactly as
nx does.

Note: ``copy.deepcopy(G)`` — Python's builtin — still routes through
``__deepcopy__`` and remains a true deep copy. The existing
TestDeepCopyIsolation cases continue to pass.

Tests
-----
New TestGraphCopyIsShallow class in
tests/python/test_attribute_access_parity.py with 19 parametrized
cases covering:
- Inner-container identity sharing for node, edge, multigraph-edge,
  and graph-level attrs (cross-checked against nx for every graph
  class).
- Top-level attr-dict independence (adding a new attr to H.nodes[n]
  must not leak into G.nodes[n]).
- Behavioral propagation: mutating the inner container on the
  copy must be visible on the original (the load-bearing nx
  contract).

Full sweep
----------
6600 passed, 0 failed (was 6581 before — +19 for the new
parametrized cases).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -14,6 +14,7 @@
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3dxfn","title":"parity: all_pairs_dijkstra_path / all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length outer-key iteration order doesn't match nx","description":"fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path and fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra_path_length yield outer-key (source) iteration in arbitrary Rust internal order (e.g. ['a','e','c','d','b']) instead of nx's node-insertion order (['c','d','a','b','e']). Drop-in code that iterates 'for source, dists in result.items(): ...' breaks. Same iteration-order family fixed for triangles/core_number/etc. Note: outer keys for all_pairs_shortest_path[_length] already match nx; only dijkstra variants need the fix.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T10:35:36.535013167Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.955576532Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T10:38:03.953597595Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 40d5417. all_pairs_dijkstra_path / _length outer keys now iterate in node-insertion order matching nx. 6 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3lqi6","title":"DiGraph.reverse() / fnx.reverse() edge + adj iteration order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.reverse() (and DiGraph.reverse() method) on a DiGraph returns a copy whose edge-iteration order and per-node adj-iteration order differ from nx.

Repro:
  edges = [('a','b'),('b','c'),('c','d')]  # path
  fnx.reverse(dg).edges() -> [('c','b'),('d','c'),('b','a')]
  nx.reverse(dgx).edges() -> [('b','a'),('c','b'),('d','c')]

  edges = [(0,1),(1,2),(2,3),(3,0)]  # cycle
  fnx -> [(1,0),(3,2),(0,3),(2,1)]
  nx  -> [(0,3),(1,0),(2,1),(3,2)]

  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','a'),('c','a')]
  fnx adj['a'] = ['d', 'c']
  nx adj['a']  = ['c', 'd']

nx iterates nodes in insertion order and emits reversed (v→u) edges in that order; fnx's Rust-side reverse traverses internal adjacency in a different order.

The copy=False path matches. The copy=True path (default for both function and method) drifts.

Fix: delegate the copy=True path to nx so the resulting DiGraph's edge + adj iteration matches nx's contract.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T13:56:39.907591704Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.487970050Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T13:59:41.485884732Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3qopf","title":"k_truss subgraph node order drifts from nx","description":"fnx.k_truss builds the resulting subgraph from Rust output which iterates nodes in canonical/sort order, while nx iterates them in input-graph node order.

Repro:
  edges = [('c','d'),('a','b'),('b','c'),('d','e'),('a','c')]
  fnx.k_truss(g, 2).nodes() -> ['a','b','c','d','e']
  nx.k_truss(gx, 2).nodes()  -> ['c','d','a','b','e']

Drop-in code that iterated R.nodes() / R.edges() / R.adj of the k-truss subgraph in nx's input-graph-order broke. Fix: delegate k_truss to nx via _call_networkx_for_parity + _from_nx_graph for output rehydration.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:37:05.403750981Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:39:25.048950577Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:39:25.046943262Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"br-r37-c1-3tlkj","title":"Graph.copy() does deep copy — nx documents and implements shallow copy","description":"fnx's Graph.copy() (and DiGraph/MultiGraph/MultiDiGraph) deep-copies node/edge/graph attributes via copy.deepcopy. nx documents and implements shallow copy: G.copy() returns a graph whose nodes/edges/graph dicts are *new* dicts but whose values (e.g. nested lists/dicts inside node attrs) are shared with the original.

Repro:
  >>> G = fnx.Graph(); G.add_node(1, attrs={'list': [1,2,3]})
  >>> H = G.copy()
  >>> H.nodes[1]['attrs'] is G.nodes[1]['attrs']  # fnx: False, nx: True
  >>> H.nodes[1]['attrs']['list'].append(99)
  >>> G.nodes[1]['attrs']['list']  # fnx: [1,2,3], nx: [1,2,3,99]

This is documented in nx.Graph.copy: 'an independent shallow copy of the graph and attributes. That is, if an attribute is a container, that container is shared by the original and the copy.'

Root cause: _copy_preserving_insertion_order in __init__.py uses deepcopy(attrs) and deepcopy(self.graph). Should mirror nx.Graph.copy's d.copy() / datadict.copy() / G.graph.update(self.graph) shallow-copy semantics.

Note: copy.deepcopy(G) (Python's built-in) MUST still do full deep copy — that path goes through __deepcopy__ which is correct. Only G.copy() needs the change.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-27T02:58:28.091312403Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T02:58:57.941825603Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-3vif2","title":"TestSigmaParity / TestOmegaParity tests assert no-fallback but br-smallrng delegates","description":"9 pre-existing test failures in test_graph_metrics.py:
- TestSigmaParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[path4, cycle4, complete4]
- TestSigmaParity::test_error_contract_matches_networkx_without_fallback[disconnected4]
- TestOmegaParity::test_matches_networkx_without_fallback[complete4]
- TestOmegaParity::test_error_contract_matches_networkx_without_fallback[path3, path4, cycle4, disconnected4]

The tests assert that fnx.sigma / fnx.omega compute the result WITHOUT delegating to nx (monkeypatched nx.sigma / nx.omega raises AssertionError if called). But br-smallrng made these wrappers delegate to nx — fnx's local loop with per-iteration derived seeds produced different numeric results than nx's stateful single-RNG approach, so the project chose delegation for exact parity.

Tests are stale — they assert the pre-br-smallrng contract.

Fix: drop test_matches_networkx_without_fallback entirely for sigma/omega (they now legitimately delegate), and remove the error_contract cases that hit the delegation path (only the cases that hit fnx's pre-checks survive).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T20:15:37.570006310Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T20:18:46.978893456Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T20:18:46.976905629Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4d97w","title":"_from_nx_graph (les_miserables / Davis / hoffman_singleton) drifts adj order from nx","description":"fnx's _from_nx_graph converter walks nx.G.edges() in nx's edge-iteration order and feeds them to add_edges_from. Because nx.G.edges() canonicalises each edge to '(first-discovered, second-discovered)' direction, fnx's resulting graph has adj order following the canonical edge traversal — NOT matching the nx graph's per-node adj insertion order.

This is the inverse of br-r37-c1-sgnab (_fnx_to_nx adj order), which we fixed using a per-node-queue topological emit. Same fix applies here in reverse direction.

Repro (les_miserables_graph delegates via _from_nx_graph):
  nx.les_miserables_graph().adj['Valjean']:
    ['Labarre','MmeMagloire','MlleBaptistine','Myriel','Marguerite','MmeDeR']
  fnx.les_miserables_graph().adj['Valjean']:
    ['Myriel','MlleBaptistine','MmeMagloire','Labarre','Marguerite','MmeDeR']

Affects every nx-to-fnx delegated graph: les_miserables_graph, davis_southern_women_graph, hoffman_singleton_graph (per their docstrings), plus algorithms that use _from_nx_graph (e.g. union, intersection, transitive_reduction, transitive_closure_dag, etc.).

Fix: route _from_nx_graph through the existing _topo_emit_edges_by_adj helper, which already does per-node-queue topological emission to preserve adj order across graph-format conversions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T16:11:40.761421100Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T16:17:23.441351537Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T16:17:23.439301764Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"br-r37-c1-4elbw","title":"parity: 4 more functions raise NotImplemented/NetworkXError with custom messages instead of nx's standard wording","description":"Continuation of br-r37-c1-yf4tf — 4 more functions diverge from nx's exact error messages: (1) condensation on undirected raises 'strongly_connected_components is not defined for undirected graphs. Use connected_components instead.'; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. (2) flow_hierarchy on undirected raises NetworkXError('flow_hierarchy requires a DiGraph'); nx raises NetworkXError('G must be a digraph in flow_hierarchy'). (3) in_degree_centrality and (4) out_degree_centrality on undirected raise custom 'is not defined for undirected graphs.' messages; nx raises 'not implemented for undirected type'. Drop-in code matching the exact text breaks. Fix: align all four to nx's exact wording.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T09:28:50.121233008Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.902270771Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T09:32:37.900331589Z","close_reason":"Fixed in commit 35bb5d3. strongly_connected_components/number_strongly/kosaraju/condensation/in_degree_centrality/out_degree_centrality/flow_hierarchy now raise with nx's exact error messages. 17 parity tests freeze the contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

@@ -23242,25 +23242,38 @@ def _copy_preserving_insertion_order(self, as_view=False):
     constructor/copy paths. Upstream NetworkX preserves the public edge
     orientation from insertion order, so rebuild via the Python edge
     view.
+
+    br-r37-c1-3tlkj: nx.Graph.copy() is a *shallow* copy — the
+    documented contract reads "if an attribute is a container, that
+    container is shared by the original and the copy." Previously
+    we used ``deepcopy`` for graph/node/edge attrs, which produced
+    independent containers (a stronger invariant than nx). Code
+    relying on shared mutation of nested attrs (a documented and
+    sometimes-load-bearing nx behavior) silently broke. Mirror nx's
+    ``dict(...)`` / ``self.graph.update(self.graph)`` semantics.
+    Note: copy.deepcopy(G) — the Python built-in — still routes
+    through ``__deepcopy__`` and remains a true deep copy.
     """
     if as_view is True:
         return _generic_filtered_graph_view(self)
 
     result = type(self)()
-    result.graph.update(deepcopy(self.graph))
+    # Shallow: result.graph is a new dict but its values are shared
+    # with self.graph (same as nx's G.graph.update(self.graph)).
+    result.graph.update(self.graph)
     result.add_nodes_from(
-        (node, deepcopy(attrs)) for node, attrs in self.nodes(data=True)
+        (node, dict(attrs)) for node, attrs in self.nodes(data=True)
     )
     if self.is_multigraph():
         # Use 4-tuples so an edge attribute literally named "key" doesn't
         # collide with add_edge's positional key parameter.
         result.add_edges_from(
-            (u, v, key, deepcopy(attrs))
+            (u, v, key, dict(attrs))
             for u, v, key, attrs in self.edges(keys=True, data=True)
         )
     else:
         result.add_edges_from(
-            (u, v, deepcopy(attrs)) for u, v, attrs in self.edges(data=True)
+            (u, v, dict(attrs)) for u, v, attrs in self.edges(data=True)
         )
     return result

@@ -706,6 +706,126 @@ def test_multigraph_edge_attr_isolation(self):
         assert MH[0][1]["a"]["data"] == [1, 2, 99]
 
 
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Regression: br-r37-c1-3tlkj — G.copy() must be SHALLOW (matches nx)
+# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+
+class TestGraphCopyIsShallow:
+    """nx.Graph.copy() docstring: 'an independent shallow copy of the
+    graph and attributes. That is, if an attribute is a container,
+    that container is shared by the original and the copy.' Pre-fix
+    fnx.G.copy() did a deep copy, which produced independent inner
+    containers — code that intentionally relied on shared mutation
+    of nested attrs (e.g. caching layers) silently broke. This test
+    cross-checks identity against nx so any future regression is
+    caught immediately.
+
+    Note: copy.deepcopy(G) — the Python builtin — must STILL deep-copy.
+    See TestDeepCopyIsolation above for that contract.
+    """
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_node_attr_inner_container_is_shared(self, cls_name):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)()
+        Gn = getattr(nx, cls_name)()
+        G.add_node(1, payload={"list": [1, 2, 3]})
+        Gn.add_node(1, payload={"list": [1, 2, 3]})
+
+        H = G.copy()
+        Hn = Gn.copy()
+
+        assert (H.nodes[1]["payload"] is G.nodes[1]["payload"]) == (
+            Hn.nodes[1]["payload"] is Gn.nodes[1]["payload"]
+        ), "node-attr container identity must match nx"
+        # And nx's contract is sharing — so both sides are True.
+        assert H.nodes[1]["payload"] is G.nodes[1]["payload"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph"])
+    def test_edge_attr_inner_container_is_shared(self, cls_name):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)()
+        Gn = getattr(nx, cls_name)()
+        G.add_edge(2, 3, payload={"list": [4, 5]})
+        Gn.add_edge(2, 3, payload={"list": [4, 5]})
+
+        H = G.copy()
+        Hn = Gn.copy()
+
+        assert (H[2][3]["payload"] is G[2][3]["payload"]) == (
+            Hn[2][3]["payload"] is Gn[2][3]["payload"]
+        )
+        assert H[2][3]["payload"] is G[2][3]["payload"]
+
+    def test_multigraph_edge_attr_inner_container_is_shared(self):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        G = fnx.MultiGraph()
+        Gn = nx.MultiGraph()
+        G.add_edge(2, 3, key="a", payload={"list": [4, 5]})
+        Gn.add_edge(2, 3, key="a", payload={"list": [4, 5]})
+
+        H = G.copy()
+        Hn = Gn.copy()
+
+        assert (H[2][3]["a"]["payload"] is G[2][3]["a"]["payload"]) == (
+            Hn[2][3]["a"]["payload"] is Gn[2][3]["a"]["payload"]
+        )
+        assert H[2][3]["a"]["payload"] is G[2][3]["a"]["payload"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_graph_attr_inner_container_is_shared(self, cls_name):
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)()
+        Gn = getattr(nx, cls_name)()
+        G.graph["names"] = ["a", "b"]
+        Gn.graph["names"] = ["a", "b"]
+
+        H = G.copy()
+        Hn = Gn.copy()
+
+        assert (H.graph["names"] is G.graph["names"]) == (
+            Hn.graph["names"] is Gn.graph["names"]
+        )
+        assert H.graph["names"] is G.graph["names"]
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_top_level_attr_dict_is_independent(self, cls_name):
+        """The OUTER attrs dict is a fresh copy — adding a new key
+        on H.nodes[n] must not appear on G.nodes[n]. Verifies the
+        copy isn't a view."""
+        G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)()
+        G.add_node(1, color="red")
+
+        H = G.copy()
+        H.nodes[1]["new_key"] = "added"
+
+        assert "new_key" not in G.nodes[1]
+        assert H.nodes[1]["new_key"] == "added"
+
+    @pytest.mark.parametrize("cls_name", ["Graph", "DiGraph", "MultiGraph", "MultiDiGraph"])
+    def test_inner_container_mutation_visible_to_original(self, cls_name):
+        """The behavioral expression of the shallow-copy contract:
+        appending to the inner list of a copied node attr propagates
+        to the original. This is what existing nx user code relies
+        on."""
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        G = getattr(fnx, cls_name)()
+        Gn = getattr(nx, cls_name)()
+        G.add_node(1, payload=[1, 2])
+        Gn.add_node(1, payload=[1, 2])
+
+        G.copy().nodes[1]["payload"].append(99)
+        Gn.copy().nodes[1]["payload"].append(99)
+
+        assert G.nodes[1]["payload"] == Gn.nodes[1]["payload"] == [1, 2, 99]
+
+
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
 # Regression: franken_networkx-degnbn — G.degree(nbunch) skips missing nodes
 # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
